title: Siphocampylus splendens
taxonomy: Siphocampylus splendens is classified within the family Campanulaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as bellflowers. This family consists of around 89 genera and approximately 2,300 species, many of which are found in the Northern Hemisphere. Siphocampylus splendens is further classified within the genus Siphocampylus, which comprises around 130 species found primarily in the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. The species Siphocampylus splendens is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and is often found growing in shaded and humid environments. Overall, the classification of Siphocampylus splendens reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations as a member of the Campanulaceae family.

It is said to be used in the treatment of various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems due to its antitussive and expectorant properties. The plant is also believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing pain and swelling.
